_description: Curveball overlay applied to routing_tree.json at 1:00 PM. Only the system_status, endpoint statuses, and override activations change. The tree structure is untouched — the runtime re-evaluates paths with the new state and users get re-routed automatically. This is the whole point of putting state in data, not code.

system_status_delta.active_alert: Severe weather + Open Door closure + BRIDGES at capacity. Triple-disruption protocol active.

open_door.note: Center of Hope absorbs overflow at reduced hours. Second Light daytime intake expanded.

second_light.note: Activated 20 surge cots for weather displacement.

safe_place_line.note: Promote to top of youth paths - 211 is overloaded.

override_activations.message_to_users: Wichita is under a severe-weather alert. If you need somewhere safe right now, a warming center is closer than any intake step.

override_activations.rule: Any leaf or message that referenced 211 Kansas is replaced with a direct line.
Youth routed to BRIDGES are auto-flagged for DCF emergency placement + CrossRoads overnight.

new_leaf_logic.escalation: DCF expects shelter presentation within 72 hrs for 4 transition-age youth. Ticket auto-files on referral.

after.note: Open Door daytime branch is closed; users see an inline banner explaining the reroute and why.

after.couch_surfing: rapid_rehousing_intake direct (paperwork by phone during closure)

live_inventory_badges: Open Door: CLOSED 48-72h (roof) - daytime ops moved to Second Light + Center of Hope
BRIDGES: FULL, 4-person waitlist, DCF 72h clock on 4 youth
Second Light: +20 surge cots for weather displacement
new_quick_actions: Warm handoff: me -> Second Light intake (bypass Open Door)

messaging_layer_changes._note: What users see at the top of every tree during the curveball window
messaging_layer_changes.banner_public: Wichita is under a severe-weather alert. Open Door is temporarily closed for repairs - if you used to go there, Second Light and Center of Hope are the open doors today. Youth under 18 in danger: call 316-262-4663 right now.
messaging_layer_changes.banner_case_manager: Triple-disruption protocol active. See live inventory for reroute rules. All Open Door referrals from before 1:00 PM have been re-routed automatically; check the referral queue.

what_did_not_change: The question prompts (users see consistent copy).
The endpoint definitions - only their status flipped.

why_this_design_wins_the_adaptability_sc.no_rewrites: We did not edit the tree. We changed state. Recovery on the 73rd hour is flipping statuses back.
why_this_design_wins_the_adaptability_sc.transparency: Every reroute carries a user-facing reason ('Open Door is closed for roof repair') - no silent redirects.
why_this_design_wins_the_adaptability_sc.deterministic_under_chaos: With 211 flooded and multiple failures stacked, the tree still produces a single named endpoint per path. No 'call around and figure it out.'
why_this_design_wins_the_adaptability_sc.youth_72h_clock: The DCF emergency ticket is auto-filed the moment a youth routes through an affected node - the clock does not wait for a human to remember.
